title: Southern Border Farmers and Ranchers Protection Act
short_title: Southern Border Farmers and Ranchers Protection Act
stage_label: Referred to the Subcommittee on Conservation, Research, and Biotechnology.
plain_summary: This bill helps farmers and ranchers in Texas counties near the Mexico border get money to fix damage to their land and equipment. The money comes from a government program that already helps farmers with environmental problems. Farmers can get one-year contracts to pay for repairs to land and farming tools that have been damaged. The program covers 39 counties along or close to the Texas-Mexico border. The goal is to fix damage that causes harm to the environment.
last_action_text: Referred to the Subcommittee on Conservation, Research, and Biotechnology.

sections.title: Section 1  Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Southern Border Farmers and Ranchers Protection Act.

sections.title: Section 2  Southern Border Initiative
sections.text: This section changes an existing law called the Food Security Act of nineteen eighty five. It adds a new program called the Southern Border Initiative. Under this program, the Secretary of Agriculture will give money to farmers and ranchers to help them fix damage to their land and farming equipment. This damage must be related to natural resource problems. The contracts for these payments will last for one year. This program only applies to land in specific Texas counties that are at or near the southern border with Mexico. These counties are Brewster, Brooks, Cameron, Crockett, Culberson, Dimmit, Duval, Edwards, El Paso, Frio, Hidalgo, Hudspeth, Jeff Davis, Jim Hogg, Jim Wells, Karnes, Kenedy, Kinney, Kleberg, La Salle, Live Oak, Maverick, McMullen, Pecos, Presidio, Reeves, Starr, Sutton, Terrell, Uvalde, Val Verde, Webb, Willacy, Zapata, and Zavala.
